visual_features.overall_style: Clean, slightly traditional marketing site with a friendly rural feel. Uses large photography, simple flat UI elements, and a restrained color palette (white, earthy brown, green, and a tomato red accent). Not minimalist in a tech/corporate sense, but visually simple and inviting.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by size, color contrast, and spatial grouping. The logo and navigation sit on a stark white band at the very top, creating a clear brand/navigation layer. Below, a two-column hero uses a bold full-bleed llama photo as the primary visual draw on the left, balanced by a semi-transparent overlay with centered heading and copy on the right. The main headline 'Llama Kisses Greet Visitors' is the top textual focal point: large type size, green color contrasting against the pale overlay. The red CTA button is the strongest accent and becomes the immediate second focal point. Navigation uses icon + label with the active/primary item ('Contact') emphasized in red while others are brown, helping users quickly identify the key action.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous whitespace in the header and around navigation items, creating a clean, airy feel. The hero right column has ample padding around text, with plenty of negative space above and below the headline and paragraph, enhancing readability. The left side is visually dense due to the large photograph, balanced by the softer, light overlay on the right. Margins between logo and nav and between nav items are moderate to large, confirming a relaxed, non-cramped layout.
unique_elements: Split hero treatment where the same background photo extends across the full width, but the right half is covered with a white semi-transparent overlay for text legibility.
Navigation items combining simple line icons with text labels, using earthy brown for default items and red for the emphasized 'Contact' link.
Script-style farm logo with a llama silhouette, conveying a rustic/handcrafted brand presence.
Hero CTA placed over the photo/overlay intersection region, visually anchored near the lower center-right of the hero area.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column grid (hero appears as a 6/6 split, with content constrained in a central max-width region).
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Standard top navigation with full-width hero split into two equal columns (image and text overlay). Content is organized in a linear top-to-bottom flow likely continuing below the hero.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top-level structure: (1) full-width white header containing logo left and horizontal nav right; (2) full-width hero section, visually divided into left photo and right text overlay. The right hero column vertically stacks heading, paragraph, and CTA button with consistent spacing. Sections feel clearly separated by background contrast and vertical spacing rather than heavy dividers.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Header content aligned on a horizontal baseline with the logo flush left and nav flush right. Within the nav, icons and labels are centered vertically and horizontally in their own hit areas. Hero text block is center-aligned (text is central within the right column with likely centered text alignment for the heading and paragraph). Button is centered in relation to the text block and spaced below the paragraph. Overall alignment uses a mix of left and centered alignment but adheres to a common vertical rhythm.
responsive_hints: Two-column hero layout suggests that on smaller viewports the layout will likely collapse into a single column with the image stacked above or below the text.
Navigation with widely spaced icon+label items may switch to a stacked or hamburger menu on mobile.
Full-bleed hero image and overlay are likely implemented with background-size: cover and a fluid container width.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: The content follows a linear top-to-bottom flow. At the top is a fixed-height header bar containing the logo on the left and a horizontal row of icon-labeled nav items on the right. Below that, the hero section spans the full width of the viewport and is visually split down the middle: the left half is dominated by a full-height photo of llamas near a fence, while the right half shows the same underlying image but covered by a white, slightly opaque overlay. Inside the right half, content is vertically stacked: a large headline, a paragraph of supporting text, then a prominent red CTA button. All elements are placed within ample padding from the edges of the column, with roughly equal top and bottom margins around the typography to maintain balance.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header occupies roughly 10–15% of the vertical viewport height, with generous top/bottom padding around the logo and navigation. The hero section consumes the majority of the visible viewport (around 70–80% of the height). Horizontally, space is split approximately 50/50 between the left photo column and the right text overlay column. Within the right column, the text block uses about the central 50–60% of the vertical space, leaving comfortable breathing room above and below, while the button sits slightly below the center, giving weight to the lower right quadrant. The image on the left fills its entire half, creating a strong visual mass that balances the more minimal right side.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately spacious layout with clear emphasis on large imagery and roomy typography. Header elements are well-spaced; nav items are not crowded. The hero text uses relatively large font sizes with generous line spacing and margins between blocks, resulting in a relaxed, easy-to-scan layout rather than information-dense content.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye starts at the logo on the top left, then moves horizontally across the navigation (particularly noticing the red 'Contact' item). From there, the strong full-bleed photo on the left hero column draws attention downward and left, then the semi-transparent overlay and large green headline on the right pull the eye back to the center-right. This creates a loose Z-pattern: top-left logo → top-right nav → left hero image → right hero headline and CTA, ultimately resting on the red 'About The Farm' button as the primary call-to-action.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons appear as solid-filled rectangles with subtle corner rounding (~4px radius). The primary CTA in the hero is a bold tomato red (#C62828) with white text, likely using a medium or semibold weight. No visible drop shadow; separation relies on color contrast and placement. Padding appears generous (approx. 12–14px vertical, 24–32px horizontal), giving a substantial click target. The button label uses sentence case and may include a » character or icon to signal continued navigation. Flat design with no gradients or bevels.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Most likely a semantic <button> or <a> element styled as a button (e.g., <a class='btn btn-primary'>) wrapped inside a layout container (like a flex or block-level element) for vertical stacking under the paragraph. For icon-enhanced CTAs, structure would be text plus an inline <span> or <svg> icon aligned via flexbox.

ui_patterns.visual_description: While not explicitly shown in this hero slice, the design language suggests simple, border- or background-defined cards: white or very light backgrounds (#FFFFFF/#F8F8F8), subtle 1px light gray border (#E5E5E5) or minimal shadow, and small corner rounding (~4px). Internal padding likely follows the same pattern as the hero text area (16–24px all around). Text within cards would follow a headline + short body pattern with enough spacing to keep a light, approachable feel.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Forms are not shown directly, but based on the flat, clean aesthetic, input fields are likely standard rectangles with soft corner rounding (~4px), 1px light gray border, and white background. Labels probably appear above fields for clarity and mobile friendliness. Focus state might use the green or red accent for the border. Error messages would use the red semantic color with small explanatory text under the field.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: A stacked layout with each field wrapped in a container (<div class='form-group'>) containing a <label> above a full-width <input> or <textarea>. Validation feedback could be a small <span class='error'> or <small> element. Forms might be grouped semantically via <fieldset> and <legend> for longer contact/booking forms, but overall would favor vertical stacking for easy scanning.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal bar on a white background with ample padding. Each nav item is an icon above a text label or side-by-side icon + text, colored in an earthy brown (#7B4B2A) for default state, with the active or highlighted item (Contact) using the red accent (#C62828). Icons are simple, likely filled/flat vector icons with no outlines. Text uses a regular or medium sans-serif weight. Items are evenly spaced across the right side of the header, and the overall look is flat and minimal without borders or separators.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Although no explicit list is visible in this slice, the style suggests medium-density lists with clear item separation via spacing rather than heavy rules. If used for activities or llama profiles, items might include a small thumbnail or icon on the left with text on the right, aligned in a simple media-object pattern. Typography would maintain comfortable line height with moderate spacing between items.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Lists are likely implemented as <ul> or <ol> with each <li> acting as a flex container aligning an image/icon and a text block (title + description). For more structured content (e.g., schedules), a responsive table or definition-list pattern (<dl>) might be used, but the overall approach would favor flexbox media objects for better responsiveness.

heading.visual_style: Headings appear as a clean, fairly neutral sans-serif with moderate stroke contrast and a friendly feel (not ultra-geometric). The large hero heading is bold, with a slightly rounded appearance and good legibility at large sizes.

body.visual_style: Body text is a straightforward, readable sans-serif with a humanist/grotesque feel and regular weight. It prioritizes clarity over personality, suitable for paragraphs and descriptive copy.

spacing_system.component_padding: Hero text container and buttons use approximately 16–24px padding on all sides, aligning with the 2x–3x base unit.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., header and hero, hero and next section) is likely 48–64px, with internal spacing between elements (headline, body, button) at 16–24px intervals.

interaction_hints: Navigation items likely have hover states changing icon and text color from brown to red or adding a subtle underline; the active item (Contact) is persistently red.
Primary buttons likely invert or darken their red background on hover (e.g., slightly darker red, minor scale-up, or subtle shadow) while keeping white text.
Hero image might be static, but subtle fade or parallax effects could be introduced on scroll or on page load without breaking the calm aesthetic.
Micro-interactions for links and buttons should be quick (150–250ms) easing transitions (ease-out) to maintain a responsive feel while reinforcing clickability.
